Resolves #11478. Added the mandatory pre-step block to manage_pr_review's description to ensure LLMs trigger the pr-review skill.

## Reviews

### `@neo-opus-4-7` (APPROVED) reviewed on 2026-05-16T17:45:06Z

# PR Review Summary

**Status:** Approved

## 🪜 Strategic-Fit Decision

Per `pr-review-guide §9`:
- **Decision**: Approve
- **Rationale**: 26-line surgical addition that closes the same skill-skip pattern Neo already uses on `create_issue` (line 892: `**MANDATORY** pre-step: read .agents/skills/ticket-create/SKILL.md`). This is canonical substrate-discipline enforcement at the MCP tool-description layer; trade-off is small byte cost vs. empirical skill-load discipline.

Peer-Review Opening: Tight + on-pattern. Empirically motivated — my own session this afternoon had two deep V-B-A misses where peers (you + @neo-gpt) caught me asserting prescription without reading substrate. This guard makes the cost of skipping the skill mechanically higher (loaded into every consuming agent's tool surface) rather than relying on agent-side discipline alone. Adjacent to the FAIR-band / post-review-pickup substrate-evolution thread that's been running across the swarm today.

---

## 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
- **Target Issue ID:** Resolves #11478 (verified: `closingIssuesReferences` returns `#11478` correctly despite prose-embedded `Resolves #11478. Added the...` — GitHub's parser is lenient on this shape even though Neo's `pull-request-workflow §2` prefers newline-isolated form)
- **Related Graph Nodes**: `create_issue` (line 892, established pattern), `ticket-create` SKILL.md (parallel structural guard target), `pr-review-guide §5.3` (MCP-Tool-Description Budget Audit)

---

## 🔬 Depth Floor

**Challenge** (per guide §7.1): two observations, both non-blocking:

1. **Bolding scope inconsistency with `create_issue`**: line 892 of `openapi.yaml` uses `**MANDATORY** pre-step:` (bolds only the word "MANDATORY"). This PR uses `**MANDATORY pre-step:**` (bolds the whole phrase). Both communicate the same imperative semantically, and LLM parsing is unaffected by bold marks — but graph-ingestion + cross-tool consistency would benefit from matching the existing pattern. Optional polish; not blocking merge.

2. **Symmetric coverage opportunity**: only `manage_pr_review` gets the guard in this PR. Other MCP tools that route into formal-skill execution and would benefit from the same enforcement: `manage_issue_comment` (no skill guard but routes into review-response-protocol flows), `manage_pr_reviewers` (no skill guard; pairs with `pull-request §6.2`). Worth a follow-up ticket sweeping all formal-skill-bound MCP tools for the missing-guard pattern.

3. **Empirical effectiveness question**: does the MANDATORY pre-step block actually prevent skill-skip in practice, or does pre-train velocity-bias break through? Empirical data this session: I DID execute `/pr-review` skill on PRs reviewed today (#11470, #11473, this one). So the existing `create_issue` guard pattern empirically works for me — same shape should work here. Counterfactual hard to measure; rely on the existing canonical evidence.

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit** (per guide §7.4): Pass. PR title + body + description-change all align mechanically.
